That would be a nice addition when ipify is configured in dashboard pages like https://gethomepage.dev/.

title: https://api.ipify.org?format=json is Not working getting error page

body: The deploy link in the readme does not work.

title: API not working https://api.ipify.org/?format=json
author: digabbar

title: javascript api code sample fails in firefox
author: youstupidgithub
body:
re: the javascript code sample on the ipify home page:

```
<script` type="application/javascript">
  function getIP(json) {
    document.write("My public IP address is: ", json.ip);
  }
</script>

<script type="application/javascript" `src="https://api.ipify.org?format=jsonp&callback=getIP"></script>

```

that exact code works in edge, chrome, brave, and safari.  but i can't get it to generate anything in firefox. (i tested it in firefox on various PCs and i verified that javascript is enabled in the test firefox browsers.) it doesn't even output the "My public IP address is:" string; let alone the ip address.

any thoughts? fixes? thank you very much!!!!!

title: Network Errors with Axios in Instagram WebView on iPhone (Mobile Safari UI/WKWebView)
author: wrenzi
body:
Hi everyone,

I'm experiencing frequent` Network Error` when using Axios to fetch data from the [ipify API](https://api.ipify.org/) within Instagram's WebView on iPhone (Mobile Safari UI/WKWebView).

Here’s the code snippet I’m using:
`const response = await axios.get('https://api.ipify.org?format=json');`

I'm implementing a retry mechanism, but I suspect this won’t fully resolve the issue. Has anyone encountered similar behavior or know what might be causing this? Any suggestions on how to address it would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help.

title: Failing SSL Handshake (sometimes, after 7/25/2024)
author: RocketPuppy
body:
I have an android emulator and phone running a react native app under which the SSL certificate provided by api.ipify.org is failing validation. The validation only fails if I set the phone date forward to 7/25/2024 (the current date is 7/18/2024). It also only fails when I make a request inside this react native app (using either XMLHTTPRequest or Fetch). Requests to api.ipify.org in chrome on the same device at the same date validate correctly. The certificate shown in chrome has the serial number `00:8E:65:1B:CF:59:EE:F8:26:11:CC:D6:43:A3:A0:F1:70`.

This is obviously very strange, and the natural suggestion is that there is a problem with my app. However, I notice that the api.ipify.org SSL certificate shown in chrome is brand new, having been issued on 7/17/2024 with a NotBefore date of Thu, 18 Jul 2024 02:50:08 GMT. Which indicates a very recent rollout of a new certificate.

Despite my best efforts I have been unable to view the certificate that is failing validation. However, all indications point me to the conclusion that I am, somehow, still getting the old certificate on this application. Was the rollout process interrupted somehow and is there still a server with the old certificate that I am somehow stuck on?

title: ipify not reachable from South Korea (and other countries)
author: vterron
body:
FYI ipify seems not to be reachable from South Korea, among a few other DNS servers.

* User report [here](https://github.com/vterron/public-ip/issues/5#issuecomment-2227576339). 
* Reproduced [here](https://github.com/vterron/public-ip/issues/5#issuecomment-2228686668) with https://www.whatsmydns.net/#A/api.ipify.org:

![2024-07-15-164630_663x595_scrot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a237a810-4c7d-41e0-87e0-76b8e86743ae)

title: another X-Forwarded-For thread
author: elagano
body:
Is it considered OK that XFF messes up ipify logic:

$ curl 'https://api.ipify.org?format=json'
{"ip":"-.-.-.60"} <-- correct IP blanked out

$ curl -H "X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1" 'https://api.ipify.org?format=json'
{"ip":"127.0.0.1"}

Asking as some user was using ipify.org output saying I do not know what I was talking about, but it is in fact ipify.org that does not know how to handle XFFs properly

At least it knows to strip out rightmost XFFs, which is good:

curl -H "X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1,255.255.255.255" 'https://api.ipify.org?format=json'
{"ip":"127.0.0.1"}


-Eric

title: Two of three endpoints (for api64) are unreachable?
author: AndreKR
body:
These are the servers I get for `api64.ipify.org`:
```
# dig aaaa api64.ipify.org @8.8.8.8

[...]

;; ANSWER SECTION:
api64.ipify.org.        300     IN      AAAA    2607:f2d8:1:3c::3
api64.ipify.org.        300     IN      AAAA    2607:f2d8:4010:51::5
api64.ipify.org.        300     IN      AAAA    2607:f2d8:2010:2::2

[...]
```

Two of those don't work for me (tried from two different servers):
```
# curl -6 -v https://api64.ipify.org/
*   Trying 2607:f2d8:4010:51::5...
```

```
# curl -6 -v https://api64.ipify.org/
*   Trying 2607:f2d8:2010:2::2...
```

One works:
```
# curl -6 -v https://api64.ipify.org/
*   Trying 2607:f2d8:1:3c::3...
* Connected to api64.ipify.org (2607:f2d8:1:3c::3) port 443 (#0)
[...]
```

title: Heroku deploy button doesn't work
author: mx1010
body: I had this app successfully setup on Heroku prior to the Sales force merger. I've since tried to deploy again using your repo button, but now it just hangs. I've tried several times. Any idea on whats going on there, or how to get around it ?

title: Waiting time too long or Bad Gateway returns
author: CCB-cerivera
body: It is currently taking too long to get the IP with this api https://api.ipify.org

title: Data Privacy Question
author: Fardone
body:
Hello,

I saw on the site that "No visitor information is ever logged. Period."  Does this mean that IP addresses captured via ipify are not storred by ipify or any third parties?  I see a [Privacy Policy](https://geo.ipify.org/privacy-policy) for **geo.ipify** that states data is collected when it is used, but understood it is a separate service.

Thanks for any clarifications.

title: unable to connect to ipify: error connecting to ipify (0)
author: KnowhereFern
body:
It seems that users are experiencing issues when trying to register or sign in to my app, but this problem occurs sporadically and only when using cellular data.

A workaround we've discovered is to instruct users to switch to a WiFi connection for the sign-in process. After this, the error does not recur.

This issue is happening on a Bubble app, utilizing the standard ipify plugin from their plugin store. This issue began recently as it has been working for the last three months fine.

title: Request are printing only ipv6
author: LuizBuffa25
body:
I am running ipify-api on my Kubernetes cluster using an external GCP load balancer.
When I send a request to my ingress that point to the ipify workload, it returns the IPV6 instead of IPV4.
If I send a request from another computer/cellphones it returns IPV4 or IPV6.

I would like to know if there is a way to run it on my infra like the public official one:
https://api.ipify.org (Always return ipv4)
ttps://api64.ipify.org (Always return ipv6)

Thanks!

title: IPv4 API Request Failure on Cellular Data
author: cw-jiwonhae
body: It appears that when using cellular data, the API request for IPv4 (http://api.ipify.org) fails to connect to its host, while it works perfectly fine when connected to a Wi-Fi network. Despite my efforts to find a solution, I have not been successful in resolving the issue.
